summary: R3's suffix-aware match `item.ID == name || strings.HasSuffix(item.ID, "-"+name)` is ambiguity-safe in the actual ready set. (1) Multi-match is structurally impossible: the ready set is built from a single `bd ready --json` against one bd database, so every ID shares one project issue-prefix (`mindspec-`). The only `-` in a bead ID separates prefix from base, so a short name matches iff it equals the ENTIRE post-prefix portion (`<base>` or `<base>.<n>`) — which is the globally-unique part of the ID. Two distinct beads cannot share that suffix, so a short name resolves to at most one bead. Empirically confirmed: name=3cj0 -> only mindspec-3cj0 (NOT mindspec-3cj0.1, which ends in `-3cj0.1`), name=3cj0.1 -> only mindspec-3cj0.1. The epic-vs-child footgun is correctly disambiguated by the `-`-prefixed suffix. Because ambiguity cannot occur, the absence of an explicit error-on-ambiguous rule is acceptable; exact-match (`item.ID == name`) is also the first disjunct, so a full-form name self-resolves before any suffix consideration. (2) Suffix guard is robust: requiring the literal `-` before name blocks partial-token false matches — name in {tz.1, cj0, j0, 1, .1} all return 0 matches (verified), so it never wrong-claims `mindspec-ettz.1` from `tz.1`. (3) No-fall-through is preserved: the spec R3 text explicitly keeps the spec-101-R1 'resolve exactly or fail, never fall through to items[0]' guarantee, and AC pins that a name in neither form still returns the not-in-ready-set error. NON-BLOCKING polish: the spec proves correctness implicitly but does not STATE the single-issue-prefix invariant as the reason multi-match is safe; one sentence noting 'the ready set carries one issue-prefix, so a post-prefix suffix is unique' would harden the rationale. Not a design defect — does not gate approval. Verified the theoretical two-prefix collision (mindspec-abcd + other-mindspec-abcd both matching) cannot arise from a single-database ready set, and cross-repo is explicitly a Non-Goal.
